tasks.prompt:
You are operating inside a controlled supervisor in WRITE MODE on the BACKEND repository at C:\dev\luxury-language-api. Your sole task is to delete the safe subset of .GOLD and .GOLD.<number> backup files.

A backup file in this repo has either of these patterns:
- Ends in literal extension ".GOLD" (e.g., "router.js.GOLD")
- Ends in ".GOLD." followed by digits (e.g., "attempt.js.GOLD.2", "migrate.js.GOLD.3")

These are leftover from past refactors.

SCOPE — files you MAY consider for deletion:
- Any file matching either pattern above, inside C:\dev\luxury-language-api
- EXCLUDE anything under node_modules\, .git\, or _ARCHIVE\ (do not even inspect these)

SAFETY CRITERIA — a backup is SAFE TO DELETE if EITHER condition is true:

(Rule A — same-state redundancy): BOTH of the following are true:
  1. The corresponding source file (the backup filename minus the .GOLD or .GOLD.<number> suffix) still exists in the same directory
  2. The source file last-modified timestamp is OLDER than or EQUAL to the backup last-modified timestamp (meaning the source has not been edited since the backup was made — the backup is redundant)

(Rule B — age-based fallback): The backup file own last-modified timestamp is OLDER than 2026-02-14. Rationale: backups older than 60 days are backed by Git history at this point — many commits have happened since they were created.

If NEITHER rule applies, KEEP the backup (do not delete).

DO NOT DELETE — under any circumstance, even if the rules above would otherwise allow it:
- Any file that does NOT match the backup patterns
- Any of these CANARY files (the spine of the backend, must never be touched):
  - api/router.js
  - lib/pool.js
  - lib/supabase.js
  - lib/voice.js
  - vercel.json
  - package.json
  - routes/evaluate.js
- Any backup whose source file is missing AND is newer than 2026-02-14
- Any backup that is newer than 2026-02-14 AND whose source has been modified since the backup was made

Note on canaries: backup versions of canary files (e.g., "router.js.GOLD") are also OFF LIMITS. Do not delete them either.

SOFT ABORT: If you compute that more than 25 backup files would be deleted by these rules, STOP. Do not delete anything. Output the count and the first 10 candidate paths so the human can review the rule for unintended breadth.

REQUIRED OUTPUT — produce a manifest with three sections:
1. DELETED: full path of each backup file you removed, one per line, with which rule (A or B) authorized the deletion
2. KEPT: full path of each backup file you did NOT remove, with a one-line reason for each (canary / source missing / source newer than backup / etc.)
3. SUMMARY: total backup files found, total deleted under Rule A, total deleted under Rule B, total kept

Do not modify any file other than deleting safe backup files. Do not create new files. Do not edit source files. Do not touch anything under node_modules, .git, or _ARCHIVE.
